entries.description: ProPublica investigation: Justice Clarence Thomas accepted undisclosed luxury trips from billionaire Harlan Crow for over two decades, including private jet and superyacht travel, violating federal disclosure laws
entries.causation_evidence: ProPublica investigative series and Senate Judiciary Committee report

entries.description: ProPublica report: Justice Samuel Alito failed to disclose 2008 luxury fishing trip on private jet paid by hedge-fund billionaire Paul Singer, who later had cases before the Court
entries.causation_evidence: ProPublica reporting and Alito's Wall Street Journal response

entries.description: Senate Judiciary Committee investigation: Justice Thomas failed to disclose additional private jet and yacht trips from Harlan Crow in 2021 plus over $4.75 million in total undisclosed gifts since 1991
Supreme Court adopts first-ever Code of Conduct but makes it non-binding with no enforcement mechanism and weakens federal recusal standard ('should' instead of 'shall')
entries.causation_evidence: Supreme Court Code of Conduct document and expert analyses (CREW, Center for American Progress)

entries.description: Judicial Conference refuses to refer ethics complaints against Justice Thomas to DOJ for investigation despite Senate requests on undisclosed gifts and real estate deals
entries.causation_evidence: SCOTUSblog reporting and Judicial Conference letters

entries.description: Federal district courts face severe understaffing: 677 judges handling over 746,000 pending cases (more than 1,000 per judge); Judicial Conference requests 71 new judgeships
entries.causation_evidence: U.S. Courts caseload statistics and Judicial Conference 2025 judgeship request

entries.description: Immigration court backlog reaches record 1.7+ million cases with average wait times of nearly 900 days (almost 2.5 years)
entries.causation_evidence: EOIR January 2026 report and TRAC Immigration Court Backlog data

entries.description: Major hack of federal courts' CM/ECF system exposes sealed documents including wiretaps and confidential informants; slow response to prior 2020 breach
entries.causation_evidence: Reuters reporting and Senate cybersecurity concerns

entries.description: Record spike in federal judicial misconduct complaints: over 1,850 filed in 2025, up 23% from prior year
entries.causation_evidence: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts 2025 statistics

entries.description: Qualified immunity doctrine shields government officials in over 5,500 federal appeals (2010-2020); granted in 54% of cases, denying civil rights remedies
entries.causation_evidence: Institute for Justice 'Unaccountable' study of 5,526 appeals

entries.description: Chronic federal court backlogs: strategic scheduling changes could reduce delays by 65%, but systemic understaffing persists nationwide
entries.causation_evidence: Phys.org / international court efficiency research applicable to U.S. appeals

entries.description: Repeated cybersecurity failures in federal court records system (2020 Russian-linked breach + 2025 hack) with slow remediation of vulnerabilities
entries.causation_evidence: New York Times investigation into CM/ECF breaches

entries.description: Judicial Conference requests creation of 71 new judgeships (2 appellate, 69 district) to address growing caseloads and backlogs
entries.causation_evidence: U.S. Courts official 2025 judgeship recommendation report

entries.description: Ongoing federal judiciary efforts to fast-track secure case management system upgrades after 2025 hack disclosure
entries.causation_evidence: Reuters reporting on Judicial Conference IT Committee plans

entries.description: Continued failure to enforce ethics reforms: multiple justices (Thomas, Alito) face unresolved recusal and disclosure issues despite public criticism
entries.causation_evidence: CREW and Brookings analyses of ongoing Supreme Court ethics crisis
